data.title: Top Wall Street Forecasters Revamp Morgan Stanley Expectations Ahead Of Q1 Earnings
data.description: Morgan Stanley (NYSE: MS) is scheduled to release its fourth-quarter earnings on April 15th. Analysts predict the company will report earnings of $3.01 per share, an increase from $2.60 per share in the previous year. The consensus estimate for quarterly revenue is $19.7 billion, up from $17.74 billion last year. On April 8th, Morgan Stanley Investment Management launched the Morgan Stanley Bitcoin Trust. Following this news, Morgan Stanley's stock rose by 1.2% to close at $178.16. For more detailed information, readers can visit the Analyst Stock Ratings page on Benzinga's website to access the latest analyst ratings.

data.title: Trump gov faces legal battle over 10% import tax imposed post Supreme Court ruling, challenged by states & small businesses.
data.description: After the Supreme Court invalidated a set of tariffs, President Trump introduced a new 10 percent tax on numerous imports. States and small businesses are now contesting this tax, which is impacting trade significantly.

data.title: Rising fuel costs drive up prices, while a volatile market curbs consumer spending in the US.
data.description: Increasing fuel expenses are causing a rise in both food and travel prices. The unstable stock market is also impacting consumer behavior, leading to a decrease in discretionary spending.

data.title: New $100,000 fee by Trump blocked some businesses from H-1B visas, disrupting the program for skilled workers.
data.description: The skilled worker program has been significantly impacted by the Trump administration's implementation of a $100,000 fee for new visas in September.

data.title: Airlines transformed first-class seats from giveaways to big moneymakers by enticing passengers to pay more for luxury.
data.description: In the past, airlines would often offer their premium seats as complimentary upgrades, but this practice has significantly shifted as companies have developed strategies to entice customers into paying high prices for these seats.

data.title: Marti Technologies, Inc. to Report 2025 Fiscal Year Results on April 13, 2026
data.description: Marti Technologies, Inc. (NYSE American: MRT), a prominent mobility super app in Turkey, is set to disclose its financial and operational outcomes for the fiscal year ending on December 31, 2025, before U.S. markets open on Monday, April 13, 2026. In a scheduled conference call and webcast, Marti's management will delve into the details of the 2025 Fiscal Year Results at 3:30 p.m. in Istanbul and 1:30 p.m. in another undisclosed location.
